threats.title: Weak Authentication Bypass
threats.description: An attacker could potentially bypass authentication mechanisms by exploiting weaknesses in the username/password authentication system. This could involve brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication library itself.  Without knowing the specifics of the authentication implementation, this is a high-priority concern.
threats.attack_vector: Brute-force attack, credential stuffing, vulnerability in authentication library, weak password policies.
mitigations: Implement Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
Enforce strong password policies (length, complexity, rotation)
Regularly audit authentication logs for suspicious activity

threats.title: Data Injection via Untrusted Input
threats.description: If the system accepts user input without proper validation and sanitization, an attacker could inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection, XSS) into the system. This could lead to data corruption, unauthorized access, or the execution of arbitrary code on the server.
threats.affected_component: All Input Handling Components (e.g., web application, API)
threats.attack_vector: Unvalidated user input, lack of input sanitization, improper escaping of characters.
mitigations: Implement robust input validation and sanitization
Use parameterized queries or prepared statements to prevent SQL injection
Employ Content Security Policy (CSP) to mitigate XSS attacks

threats.title: Data Leakage due to Inadequate Access Controls
threats.description: If access controls are not properly configured, unauthorized users could potentially access sensitive data. This could occur due to misconfigured permissions, vulnerabilities in the access control mechanism, or lack of data classification.
threats.attack_vector: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference), misconfigured permissions, vulnerabilities in access control libraries.
mitigations: Implement strict access controls based on the principle of least privilege
Regularly review and update access permissions
Enforce data classification policies and implement appropriate security controls based on data sensitivity

threats.title: Lack of Audit Logging
threats.description: Without comprehensive audit logging, it becomes difficult to track user actions, identify security incidents, and determine accountability. This significantly hinders incident response and forensic investigations.  The absence of detailed logs makes it impossible to prove or disprove a user's involvement in a malicious activity.
threats.attack_vector: Lack of logging mechanisms, intentional log tampering (if possible)
mitigations: Implement detailed audit logging of all critical system events
Securely store audit logs to prevent tampering
Regularly review audit logs for suspicious activity

threats.title: Resource Exhaustion via Malicious Input
threats.description: An attacker could potentially trigger a denial-of-service attack by sending a large volume of requests to the system or by injecting malicious data that causes the system to consume excessive resources (e.g., CPU, memory, network bandwidth).
threats.affected_component: Application Servers, Database Servers, Network Infrastructure
threats.attack_vector: Malformed requests, oversized data payloads, resource-intensive operations triggered by user input.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting to prevent excessive requests
Validate and sanitize user input to prevent resource-intensive operations
Utilize a web application firewall (WAF) to filter malicious traffic

gaps: Insufficient coverage of AC controls beyond basic access control.
Limited implementation of SI controls related to input validation and data leakage.
Lack of comprehensive logging and auditing practices beyond basic requirements.
priorities: High: Weak Authentication Bypass (AC-17, AU-2, AU-3, IA-2) - Critical severity, impacting all systems.
Medium: Data Injection via Untrusted Input (AU-9, SC-28, SI-10) - High severity, potential for significant data compromise.

dfd_mermaid:
flowchart TB

    %% Styling
    classDef external fill:#e1f5fe,stroke:#0277bd,stroke-width:2px
    classDef process fill:#e8f5e9,stroke:#2e7d32,stroke-width:2px
    classDef datastore fill:#fff3e0,stroke:#ef6c00,stroke-width:2px
    classDef threat fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px

    subgraph External["External Entities"]
        user[("👤 User")]
        admin[("👔 Admin")]
        external_api[("🌐 External API")]
    end

    subgraph TrustBoundary["Trust Boundary"]
        subgraph Frontend["Frontend Layer"]
            web_app["🖥️ Web Application"]
        end

        subgraph Backend["Backend Layer"]
            api_gateway["🚪 API Gateway"]
            auth_service["🔐 Auth Service"]
            core_api["⚙️ Core API"]
        end

        subgraph Data["Data Layer"]
            database[("💾 Database")]
            cache[("⚡ Cache")]
        end
    end

    %% Data Flows
    user -->|"🔒 HTTPS"| web_app
    admin -->|"🔒 HTTPS + MFA"| web_app
    web_app -->|"🔒 API Calls"| api_gateway
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Auth Check"| auth_service
    api_gateway -->|"🔒 Business Logic"| core_api
    auth_service -->|"🔒 User Data"| database
    core_api -->|"🔒 CRUD"| database
    core_api -->|"⚠️ Session"| cache
    core_api -->|"🔒 External Call"| external_api

    %% Apply Styles
    class user,admin,external_api external
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,core_api process
    class database,cache datastore
    class web_app,api_gateway,auth_service,database threat

custom_rules.impact: An attacker could potentially bypass authentication mechanisms by exploiting weaknesses in the username/password authentication system. This could involve brute-force attacks, credential stuffing, or exploiting vulnerabilities in the authentication library itself.  Without knowing the specifics of the authentication implementation, this is a high-priority concern.

If the system accepts user input without proper validation and sanitization, an attacker could inject malicious code (e.g., SQL injection, XSS) into the system. This could lead to data corruption, unauthorized access, or the execution of arbitrary code on the server.
custom_rules.resolution: Implement robust input validation and sanitization

custom_rules.description: Data Leakage due to Inadequate Access Controls
custom_rules.impact: If access controls are not properly configured, unauthorized users could potentially access sensitive data. This could occur due to misconfigured permissions, vulnerabilities in the access control mechanism, or lack of data classification.
custom_rules.resolution: Implement strict access controls based on the principle of least privilege

custom_rules.impact: Without comprehensive audit logging, it becomes difficult to track user actions, identify security incidents, and determine accountability. This significantly hinders incident response and forensic investigations.  The absence of detailed logs makes it impossible to prove or disprove a user's involvement in a malicious activity.
custom_rules.resolution: Implement detailed audit logging of all critical system events

custom_rules.impact: An attacker could potentially trigger a denial-of-service attack by sending a large volume of requests to the system or by injecting malicious data that causes the system to consume excessive resources (e.g., CPU, memory, network bandwidth).
custom_rules.resolution: Implement rate limiting to prevent excessive requests

  message: Weak Authentication Bypass
  metadata:

    confidence: HIGH
    cwe: CWE-287
    owasp: A07:2021
    threat_category: Spoofing
  patterns:
  - pattern: password = "..."
  - pattern: secret = "..."
  - pattern: api_key = "..."
  severity: ERROR
- fix: cursor.execute($QUERY, (params,))
  id: security-custom-2

  severity: ERROR
